gaps.snippet: # [doc = " An affine open-file handle (LR-8: consumed exactly once)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Obtained from [`Fs::open`]. A future Mycelium-lang implementation can enforce it at"] # [doc = " compile time (RFC-0006 LR-8). Until then, every op checks `consumed` first."] # [derive (Debug)] pub struct File { handle : InMemHandle , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The filesystem context: holds the substrate and exposes all effectful fs ops."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " All effectful operations (`open`, `stat`, `read_dir`, `create_dir`, `remove_file`,"] # [doc = " `remove_dir`, `rename`, `copy`) are methods on `Fs`. This makes the filesystem effect"] # [doc = " explicit and inspectable: a function that does not hold an `&mut Fs` cannot perform"] # [doc = " filesystem IO (partial C6 capability scoping — see FLAG Q5)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Currently backed by `InMemoryFs`. Wire `RealFs` (M-541) when the `std-sys` phylum lands."] pub struct Fs { backend : InMemoryFs , }

gaps.snippet: impl Fs { # [doc = " Create a new `Fs` over a fresh in-memory substrate."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none (pure construction)"] # [doc = " The in-memory substrate is fully testable without OS interaction."] # [must_use] pub fn in_memory () -> Self { Self { backend : InMemoryFs :: new () , } } # [doc = " Create a new `Fs` with a simulated disk limit (for testing `DiskFull` paths)."] # [must_use] pub fn in_memory_with_limit (bytes : u64) -> Self { Self { backend : InMemoryFs :: new () . with_disk_limit (bytes) , } } # [doc = " Construct a `Path` from a UTF-8 string."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee: `Exact`, total, effects: none"] # [must_use] pub fn path (& self , s : impl Into < String >) -> Path { Path :: new (s) } # [doc = " Check whether a path exists."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee: `Exact`, `Result<bool, FsErr>`"] # [doc = " # Effects: io"] # [doc = " # `wild`?: yes (real-OS: `stat`/`access` syscall; in-memory: map lookup)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns `Err(PermDenied)` when the OS denies access to the path. **Never** returns `Ok(false)`"] # [doc = " as a substitute for a permission failure (C1: no silent clamp)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG (Q5): the `io` effect is currently ambient on this `&mut Fs`; the capability-scoped"] # [doc = " design (WASI-style) is deferred (spec §7-Q5)."] pub fn exists (& self , path : & Path) -> Result < bool , FsErr > { self . backend . exists (path . as_str ()) } # [doc = " Get filesystem metadata for a path."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee: `Exact`, `Result<Metadata, FsErr>`"] # [doc = " # Effects: io"] # [doc = " # `wild`?: yes (real-OS: `stat`/`fstat` syscall)"] # [doc = " # Errors: `NotFound` | `PermDenied`"] pub fn stat (& self , path : & Path) -> Result < Metadata , FsErr > { self . backend . stat (path . as_str ()) } # [doc = " Open a path to an affine `File` handle under an explicit `OpenOptions`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee: `Exact`, `Result<File, FsErr>`"] # [doc = " # Effects: io"] # [doc = " # `wild`?: yes (real-OS: `open`/`openat` syscall)"] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - `NotFound` — path absent and `create`/`create_new` not set (**no silent create**, C1)"] # [doc = " - `AlreadyExists` — `create_new` set and path exists (**no silent overwrite**, C1)"] # [doc = " - `PermDenied` — OS access denied"] # [doc = " - `IsADirectory` — path is a directory; open a file with `read_dir` for directories"] # [doc = " - `NotFound` — parent directory does not exist (create/create_new path)"] pub fn open (& mut self , path : & Path , opts : & OpenOptions) -> Result < File , FsErr > { let handle = self . backend . open (path . as_str () , opts) ? ; Ok (File { handle }) } # [doc = " Read bytes from an open `File` handle into `buf`. Returns the bytes read."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A short read (fewer than `buf.len()` bytes) is returned explicitly — not swallowed (C1)."] # [doc = " `0` means EOF."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee: `Exact`, `Result<usize, FsErr>`"] # [doc = " # Effects: io (io M-514 seam — FLAG Q2)"] # [doc = " # `wild`?: yes (real-OS: `read` syscall)"] # [doc = " # Errors: `UseAfterConsume` | `PermDenied` | `NotFound` | `IsADirectory`"] pub fn read (& self , file : & mut File , buf : & mut [u8]) -> Result < usize , FsErr > { self . backend . read (& mut file . handle , buf) } # [doc = " Write bytes to an open `File` handle. Returns the bytes written."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A short write (fewer than `buf.len()` bytes) is returned explicitly — never swallowed (C1)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee: `Exact`, `Result<usize, FsErr>`"] # [doc = " # Effects: io (io M-514 seam — FLAG Q2)"] # [doc = " # `wild`?: yes (real-OS: `write` syscall)"] # [doc = " # Errors: `UseAfterConsume` | `PermDenied` | `DiskFull` | `NotFound`"] pub fn write (& mut self , file : & mut File , buf : & [u8]) -> Result < usize , FsErr > { self . backend . write (& mut file . handle , buf) } # [doc = " Flush deferred write state for a `File` handle."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " In the real-OS path this calls `fsync`. In the in-memory substrate it is a consistency"] # [doc = " check (still surfaces `UseAfterConsume` if the handle is consumed)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee: `Exact`, `Result<(), FsErr>`"] # [doc = " # Effects: io (io M-514 seam — FLAG Q2)"] # [doc = " # `wild`?: yes (real-OS: `fsync`/`write` syscall)"] # [doc = " # Errors: `UseAfterConsume` | `Io` | `DiskFull`"] pub fn flush (& self , file : & File) -> Result < () , FsErr > { self . backend . flush (& file . handle) } # [doc = " Close (consume) a `File` handle. **This consumes the handle (LR-8).**"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " After `close`, any subsequent use of `file` returns `Err(UseAfterConsume)`. A close error"] # [doc = " is surfaced, not dropped (C1)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee: `Exact`, `Result<(), FsErr>`"] # [doc = " # Effects: io"] # [doc = " # `wild`?: yes (real-OS: `close` syscall)"] # [doc = " # Errors: `UseAfterConsume` | `Io` | `DiskFull`"] pub fn close (& self , file : & mut File) -> Result < () , FsErr > { self . backend . close (& mut file . handle) } # [doc = " List the entries in a directory."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns a `DirIter` that yields child path strings. The iterator is a snapshot — it is not"] # [doc = " backed by a live `readdir` stream (in the in-memory substrate; the real-OS seam will differ"] # [doc = " once M-541 lands)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee: `Exact`, `Result<DirIter, FsErr>`"] # [doc = " # Effects: io"] # [doc = " # `wild`?: yes (real-OS: `opendir`/`readdir` syscall)"] # [doc = " # Errors: `NotFound` | `NotADirectory` | `PermDenied`"] pub fn read_dir (& self , path : & Path) -> Result < DirIter , FsErr > { let entries = self . backend . read_dir (path . as_str ()) ? ; Ok (DirIter { entries : entries . into_iter () , }) } # [doc = " Create a directory at `path`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee: `Exact`, `Result<(), FsErr>`"] # [doc = " # Effects: io"] # [doc = " # `wild`?: yes (real-OS: `mkdir` syscall)"] # [doc = " # Errors: `AlreadyExists` | `PermDenied` | `NotFound` (parent absent)"] pub fn create_dir (& mut self , path : & Path) -> Result < () , FsErr > { self . backend . create_dir (path . as_str ()) } # [doc = " Remove a regular file at `path`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee: `Exact`, `Result<(), FsErr>`"] # [doc = " # Effects: io"] # [doc = " # `wild`?: yes (real-OS: `unlink` syscall)"] # [doc = " # Errors: `NotFound` | `PermDenied` | `IsADirectory` (use `remove_dir` for directories)"] pub fn remove_file (& mut self , path : & Path) -> Result < () , FsErr > { self . backend . remove_file (path . as_str ()) } # [doc = " Remove an **empty** directory at `path`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee: `Exact`, `Result<(), FsErr>`"] # [doc = " # Effects: io"] # [doc = " # `wild`?: yes (real-OS: `rmdir` syscall)"] # [doc = " # Errors: `NotEmpty` | `NotFound` | `PermDenied`"] pub fn remove_dir (& mut self , path : & Path) -> Result < () , FsErr > { self . backend . remove_dir (path . as_str ()) } # [doc = " Rename / move `from` to `to`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **No silent overwrite**: if `to` already exists, returns `Err(AlreadyExists)` (C1)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee: `Exact`, `Result<(), FsErr>`"] # [doc = " # Effects: io"] # [doc = " # `wild`?: yes (real-OS: `rename`/`renameat` syscall)"] # [doc = " # Errors: `NotFound` | `PermDenied` | `CrossDevice` | `AlreadyExists`"] # [doc = " # FLAG (Q4): cross-device rename atomicity is unsettled; in-memory ignores it."] pub fn rename (& mut self , from : & Path , to : & Path) -> Result < () , FsErr > { self . backend . rename (from . as_str () , to . as_str ()) } # [doc = " Copy `from` to `to`. gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; fn fs () -> Fs { Fs :: in_memory () } # [doc = " Full create-write-close-read round-trip over the public API."] # [test] fn create_write_read_round_trip () { let mut fs = fs () ; let p = Path :: new ("/hello.txt") ; let opts_w = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; let mut file = fs . open (& p , & opts_w) . expect ("open for write") ; let n = fs . write (& mut file , b"hello world") . expect ("write") ; assert_eq ! (n , 11) ; fs . close (& mut file) . expect ("close") ; let opts_r = OpenOptions :: read_only () ; let mut rfile = fs . open (& p , & opts_r) . expect ("open for read") ; let mut buf = vec ! [0u8 ; 16] ; let rn = fs . read (& mut rfile , & mut buf) . expect ("read") ; assert_eq ! (rn , 11) ; assert_eq ! (& buf [.. 11] , b"hello world") ; fs . close (& mut rfile) . expect ("close") ; } # [doc = " `stat` returns correct metadata after creating a file."] # [test] fn stat_returns_correct_metadata () { let mut fs = fs () ; let p = Path :: new ("/meta.txt") ; let opts_w = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; let mut h = fs . open (& p , & opts_w) . expect ("open") ; fs . write (& mut h , b"abc") . expect ("write") ; fs . close (& mut h) . expect ("close") ; let meta = fs . stat (& p) . expect ("stat") ; assert ! (meta . is_file ()) ; assert_eq ! (meta . size , 3) ; } # [doc = " `exists` is `true` after creation, `false` before."] # [test] fn exists_reflects_creation_and_removal () { let mut fs = fs () ; let p = Path :: new ("/exist_test.txt") ; assert ! (! fs . exists (& p) . expect ("exists before create")) ; let opts_w = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; let mut h = fs . open (& p , & opts_w) . expect ("open") ; fs . close (& mut h) . expect ("close") ; assert ! (fs . exists (& p) . expect ("exists after create")) ; fs . remove_file (& p) . expect ("remove") ; assert ! (! fs . exists (& p) . expect ("exists after remove")) ; } # [doc = " Opening an absent file without create returns `NotFound` (no silent create, C1)."] # [doc = " Guard: returning Ok or a default handle makes this fail."] # [test] fn open_absent_without_create_returns_not_found () { let mut fs = fs () ; let p = Path :: new ("/no-such.txt") ; let err = fs . open (& p , & OpenOptions :: read_only ()) . expect_err ("must fail") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , FsErr :: NotFound { .. }) , "expected NotFound; got {err:?}") ; } # [doc = " `create_new` on an existing file returns `AlreadyExists` (no silent overwrite, C1)."] # [test] fn create_new_on_existing_returns_already_exists () { let mut fs = fs () ; let p = Path :: new ("/dup.txt") ; let opts_c = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; let mut h = fs . open (& p , & opts_c) . expect ("create") ; fs . close (& mut h) . expect ("close") ; let opts_cn = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create_new (true) ; let err = fs . open (& p , & opts_cn) . expect_err ("must fail") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , FsErr :: AlreadyExists { .. }) , "expected AlreadyExists; got {err:?}") ; } # [doc = " Use-after-consume returns `UseAfterConsume` (LR-8 — never UB)."] # [doc = " Guard: returning Ok or panicking on a consumed handle makes this fail."] # [test] fn use_after_consume_is_explicit_error () { let mut fs = fs () ; let p = Path :: new ("/consumed.txt") ; let opts_w = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; let mut h = fs . open (& p , & opts_w) . expect ("open") ; fs . close (& mut h) . expect ("close") ; let err = fs . write (& mut h , b"data") . expect_err ("must fail") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , FsErr :: UseAfterConsume { .. }) , "use after consume must return UseAfterConsume (LR-8); got {err:?}") ; } # [doc = " Rename to an existing target returns `AlreadyExists` (no silent overwrite, C1)."] # [test] fn rename_no_silent_overwrite () { let mut fs = fs () ; let opts_c = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; let a = Path :: new ("/a.txt") ; let b = Path :: new ("/b.txt") ; let mut ha = fs . open (& a , & opts_c) . expect ("create a") ; fs . close (& mut ha) . expect ("close") ; let mut hb = fs . open (& b , & opts_c . clone ()) . expect ("create b") ; fs . close (& mut hb) . expect ("close") ; let err = fs . rename (& a , & b) . expect_err ("must fail") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , FsErr :: AlreadyExists { .. }) , "rename to existing must return AlreadyExists; got {err:?}") ; } # [doc = " Copy to an existing target returns `AlreadyExists` (no silent overwrite, C1)."] # [test] fn copy_no_silent_overwrite () { let mut fs = fs () ; let opts_c = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; let a = Path :: new ("/src.txt") ; let b = Path :: new ("/dst.txt") ; let mut ha = fs . open (& a , & opts_c) . expect ("create src") ; fs . close (& mut ha) . expect ("close") ; let mut hb = fs . open (& b , & opts_c . clone ()) . expect ("create dst") ; fs . close (& mut hb) . expect ("close") ; let err = fs . copy (& a , & b) . expect_err ("must fail") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , FsErr :: AlreadyExists { .. }) , "copy to existing must return AlreadyExists; got {err:?}") ; } # [doc = " Truncate without write is rejected at validation time (no silent data loss)."] # [test] fn truncate_without_write_is_rejected () { let opts = OpenOptions :: new () . with_truncate (true) ; assert ! (opts . validate () . is_err () , "truncate without write must be invalid (C1 — no silent truncation)") ; } # [doc = " DiskFull is an explicit error (no silent partial write, C1)."] # [test] fn disk_full_is_explicit_error () { let mut fs = Fs :: in_memory_with_limit (3) ; let p = Path :: new ("/big.txt") ; let opts_w = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; let mut h = fs . open (& p , & opts_w) . expect ("open") ; let err = fs . write (& mut h , b"hello") . expect_err ("must fail") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , FsErr :: DiskFull { .. }) , "disk full must return DiskFull; got {err:?}") ; fs . close (& mut h) . ok () ; } # [doc = " Creating and listing a directory works end-to-end."] # [test] fn create_dir_and_list () { let mut fs = fs () ; let dir = Path :: new ("/mydir") ; fs . create_dir (& dir) . expect ("mkdir") ; let p = Path :: new ("/mydir/f.txt") ; let opts_w = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; let mut h = fs . open (& p , & opts_w) . expect ("create file in dir") ; fs . close (& mut h) . expect ("close") ; let entries : Vec < String > = fs . read_dir (& dir) . expect ("read_dir") . collect () ; assert ! (entries . contains (& "/mydir/f.txt" . to_owned ()) , "directory must list the created file") ; } # [doc = " `remove_dir` on a non-empty directory returns `NotEmpty` (C1)."] # [test] fn remove_dir_nonempty_is_error () { let mut fs = fs () ; let dir = Path :: new ("/full-dir") ; fs . create_dir (& dir) . expect ("mkdir") ; let child = Path :: new ("/full-dir/child.txt") ; let opts_w = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; let mut h = fs . open (& child , & opts_w) . expect ("create child") ; fs . close (& mut h) . expect ("close") ; let err = fs . remove_dir (& dir) . expect_err ("must fail") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , FsErr :: NotEmpty { .. }) , "expected NotEmpty; got {err:?}") ; } # [doc = " `read_dir` on a file returns `NotADirectory` (C1)."] # [test] fn read_dir_on_file_is_error () { let mut fs = fs () ; let p = Path :: new ("/just-a-file.txt") ; let opts_w = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; let mut h = fs . open (& p , & opts_w) . expect ("create") ; fs . close (& mut h) . expect ("close") ; let err = fs . read_dir (& p) . expect_err ("must fail") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , FsErr :: NotADirectory { .. }) , "expected NotADirectory; got {err:?}") ; } # [doc = " Path join and parent are value-semantic and deterministic (Exact, total)."] # [test] fn path_ops_are_deterministic () { let base = Path :: new ("/root") ; for _ in 0 .. 3 { assert_eq ! (base . join ("child") , base . join ("child") , "join must be deterministic") ; } assert_eq ! (base . join ("child") . parent () , Some (base . clone ())) ; } # [doc = " A short read (less than buf.len()) returns the actual count — never silently zero-padded."] # [doc = " Guard: zero-padding the buffer and returning buf.len() makes this fail."] # [test] fn short_read_is_explicit () { let mut fs = fs () ; let p = Path :: new ("/short.txt") ; let opts_w = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; let mut hw = fs . open (& p , & opts_w) . expect ("create") ; fs . write (& mut hw , b"hi") . expect ("write 2 bytes") ; fs . close (& mut hw) . expect ("close") ; let opts_r = OpenOptions :: read_only () ; let mut hr = fs . open (& p , & opts_r) . expect ("open for read") ; let mut buf = vec ! [0u8 ; 16] ; let n = fs . read (& mut hr , & mut buf) . expect ("read") ; assert_eq ! (n , 2 , "short read must return 2, not 16 (the buffer size)") ; assert_eq ! (& buf [.. 2] , b"hi") ; fs . close (& mut hr) . expect ("close") ; } # [doc = " The guarantee matrix covers all spec §3 ops."] # [test] fn guarantee_matrix_is_complete () { assert_eq ! (MATRIX . len () , 16 , "spec §3 lists 16 rows; matrix must match") ; } # [doc = " Every row in the matrix is Exact (VR-5 — fs has no accuracy semantics)."] # [test] fn guarantee_matrix_all_rows_exact () { for row in MATRIX { assert_eq ! (row . guarantee , "Exact" , "op {:?} must be Exact" , row . op) ; } } # [doc = " Every fallible IO row carries a non-empty error_set (C1 — never-silent contract)."] # [test] fn guarantee_matrix_fallible_rows_have_error_sets () { for row in MATRIX { if row . fallibility == Fallibility :: ResultFallible { assert ! (! row . error_set . is_empty () , "fallible op {:?} must name its error set (C1)" , row . op) ; } } } # [doc = " Append mode writes to the end of an existing file (not the beginning)."] # [test] fn append_mode_writes_at_end () { let mut fs = fs () ; let p = Path :: new ("/append_test.txt") ; let opts_w = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; let mut h1 = fs . open (& p , & opts_w) . expect ("create") ; fs . write (& mut h1 , b"hello") . expect ("write first") ; fs . close (& mut h1) . expect ("close") ; let opts_a = OpenOptions :: new () . with_append (true) ; let mut h2 = fs . open (& p , & opts_a) . expect ("open for append") ; fs . write (& mut h2 , b" world") . expect ("append") ; fs . close (& mut h2) . expect ("close") ; let opts_r = OpenOptions :: read_only () ; let mut h3 = fs . open (& p , & opts_r) . expect ("open for read") ; let mut buf = vec ! [0u8 ; 32] ; let n = fs . read (& mut h3 , & mut buf) . expect ("read") ; fs . close (& mut h3) . expect ("close") ; assert_eq ! (& buf [.. n] , b"hello world" , "append must produce 'hello world'") ; } # [doc = " After rename, source is gone and target has the original content."] # [test] fn rename_moves_content () { let mut fs = fs () ; let src = Path :: new ("/src_rename.txt") ; let dst = Path :: new ("/dst_rename.txt") ; let opts_w = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; let mut h = fs . open (& src , & opts_w) . expect ("create") ; fs . write (& mut h , b"moved") . expect ("write") ; fs . close (& mut h) . expect ("close") ; fs . rename (& src , & dst) . expect ("rename") ; assert ! (! fs . exists (& src) . unwrap () , "source must be gone after rename") ; assert ! (fs . exists (& dst) . unwrap () , "dest must exist after rename") ; let opts_r = OpenOptions :: read_only () ; let mut rh = fs . open (& dst , & opts_r) . expect ("open renamed") ; let mut buf = vec ! [0u8 ; 8] ; let n = fs . read (& mut rh , & mut buf) . expect ("read") ; fs . close (& mut rh) . expect ("close") ; assert_eq ! (& buf [.. n] , b"moved") ; } }
